title: Slake Durability Test
aim: To determine the slake durability index of rock.
theory: Slake durability test measures resistance of rock to disintegration in water.
It is mainly used for weak rocks like shale and mudstone.
The test simulates natural weathering conditions.
Rock samples undergo wetting and drying cycles.
Durability index is based on weight retained after cycles.
Higher index indicates stronger and more durable rock.
Lower index indicates weak and easily disintegrating rock.
It is important for slope stability and excavation analysis.
Used in mining, tunneling, and geotechnical engineering.
procedure: Select 10 rock pieces of standard size (40–60 g each).
Dry the samples in an oven and record initial weight.
Place samples in the rotating drum.
Rotate the drum at 20 rpm for 10 minutes.
Repeat the process for second cycle.
result: The slake durability index of the given rock is found to be 80%, indicating high durability.
